data.title: OpenAI surge in users, valuation likely to benefit Microsoft: Jefferies
data.description:
The recent gains in OpenAI's user base and valuation after its last funding round are likely to be a boon for Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), which has a significant stake in the ChatGPT maker, Jefferies said.

"We believe recent surge in ChatGPT users and OpenAI's new funding round benefit MSFT given this surge boosts OpenAI's growth, compute needs, and valuation, of which MSFT has a rev share, cloud provider role, and ownership stake," analyst Brent Thill wrote in a note to clients. Thill has a Buy rating and $500 price target on Microsoft.

OpenAI disclosed this week that it now has 500M users who use ChatGPT every week, up from 400M in February. As such, that likely means "surging" ChatGPT growth and revenue growth for OpenAI, Thill added. Media reports this week indicated that OpenAI is likely generating at least $415M in revenue per month, up from at least $333M per month at the end of last year, a gain of 30%.

In addition, Thill said Microsoft could get a potential boost for its 365 Copilot offering, as reasoning features, like Researcher, are infused inside of it, allowing it to compete with the competition. 

"M365 Copilot momentum has lagged due to lack of reasoning capabilities with products like OpenAI's Deep Research and Manus AI garnering lots of attention," Thill added. "While MSFT released Think Deeper (which gives access to OpenAI's o1 reasoning model) to Copilot users 2 months ago, our enterprise contacts indicate that it has yet to be fully released into enterprise. We believe that as these capabilities get infused/rolled out in enterprise M365 Copilot (with agents like Researcher and Analyst), it will put a damping on any convos that MSFT customers may be having internally on alternative products to M365 Copilot which have reasoning capabilities, as Copilot will now address these use cases. In our view, as enterprise rolls out reasoning capabilities within M365 Copilot, this could help to reignite MSFT's momentum."

data.title: Biogen’s tau targeting Alzheimer’s disease therapy gets FDA Fast Track status
data.description:
Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB) said on Wednesday that the U.S. FDA has granted Fast Track designation to BIIB080, its antisense oligonucleotide therapy targeting tau for Alzheimer’s disease treatment.

In December 2019, Biogen secured an exclusive global license from Ionis Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:IONS) to develop and market BIIB080, with royalty payments included.

Fast-track designation allows a faster timeline for the development and review of drugs targeted at serious conditions with unmet medical needs. It enables developers to communicate frequently with the regulator on plans for clinical studies.    

BIIB080 is an experimental treatment for Alzheimer's disease that targets tau proteins, which are linked to the disease's progression. It is currently being studied in a global Phase 2 trial called CELIA.

The Phase 2 CELIA study is fully enrolled, with a data readout expected in 2026, the company said.

data.title: The Marketing Alliance launches share repurchase program
data.description:
• The Marketing Alliance (OTCPK:MAAL) has authorized a share repurchase program to buyback up to 800,000 shares of the company's issued and outstanding common stock, effective immediately and concluding March 31, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Saipem secures $720M for offshore work in Middle East, Guyana
data.description:
Saipem (OTCPK:SAPMF) (OTCPK:SAPMY) said Wednesday it won two new offshore contracts in Middle East and Guyana worth ~$720M.

Saipem (OTCPK:SAPMF) (OTCPK:SAPMY) said the first contract encompasses engineering, procurement, construction and installation activities for the repair of damaged subsea pipelines for an unnamed client in the Middle East, with the duration of the project expected to last three years.

The second contract is a limited notice to proceed by Exxon Mobil's Guyana business and involves the EPCI of subsea structures, umbilicals, risers and flowlines for the production facility and gas export system of the proposed Hammerhead project; duration is expected to last four years.

Separately, Saipem (OTCPK:SAPMF) (OTCPK:SAPMY) said it hired four platform supply vessels to provide pipehaul support for Equinor's Irpa development offshore Norway.

data.title: Organovo reports $11.3M cash balance in FY24
data.description:
• Organovo (NASDAQ:ONVO) said on Wednesday that its preliminary cash and cash equivalents balance was about $11.3M as of the end of the fiscal year on March 31, 2025.
  • Organovo’s preliminary net cash utilization during the fourth quarter from January 1, 2025 to March 31, 2025 was about $2.0M - $2.2M.
  • The company also clarified it expects to meet all requirements for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market. Its common stock has closed above the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement since March 21, 2025 and believes it has met the minimum stockholder equity requirements of continued listing, pending Nasdaq confirmation.
  • Organovo further expects to receive a $5M milestone payment within the next 12 months associated with the anticipated start of a Phase 2 clinical trial for the FXR agonist that it recently sold. In connection with the sale of the FXR agonist, the company may receive future milestones of up to an aggregate of $50M, which is inclusive of the $5M milestone payment that would be received upon the commencement of a Phase 2 clinical trial.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Workhorse Group regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Workhorse Group (NASDAQ:WKHS) on Wednesday said it received a Nasdaq notification, informing that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement rule of $1.00 per share for continued listing.
  • As previously announced, the company effected a 1-for-12.5 reverse stock split of its common stock on March 17, 2025, intended to increase the market price of its common stock.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Proficient Auto Logistics acquires Brothers Auto Transport
data.description:
• Proficient Auto Logistics (NASDAQ:PAL) on Wednesday announced the acquisition of Brothers Auto Transport, a carrier based in Wind Gap, Pennsylvania.
  • Terms of the deal were not disclosed.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OSI Systems receives $4M award for patient monitoring solutions
data.description:
• OSI Systems (NASDAQ:OSIS) on Wednesday said its Healthcare division, Spacelabs Healthcare, is expanding its footprint with an existing U.S.-based customer under an agreement to provide approximately $4 million of patient monitoring solutions and related supplies and accessories to the hospital.
  • OSIS -0.7% premarket to $193.33.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Allakos surges on reverse merger deal with Concentra Biosciences
data.description:
• Nanocap biotech Allakos (NASDAQ:ALLK) announced a merger agreement on Wednesday to be acquired by privately held Concentra Biosciences, LLC, for $0.33 per share in cash as part of a deal expected to close in May.
  • Shares of San Carlos, California-based biotech climbed ~46% after the announcement. 
  • Per the terms, a subsidiary of Concentra is scheduled to begin a tender offer by Apr. 15 to buy all outstanding shares of Allakos (NASDAQ:ALLK) common stock.
  • “Allakos’ Board of Directors has unanimously determined that the acquisition by Concentra is in the best interests of all Allakos shareholders,” the company said.
  • ALLK added that its officers, directors, and their related parties, representing nearly 8% of the company’s common stock, have signed agreements in favor of the deal and pledged to tender their shares when the offer begins.

data.title: ConnectM receives buyout offer from its institutional investors
data.description:
• ConnectM Technology (NASDAQ:CNTM) said on Wednesday that it has received a non-binding proposal offering $1.60 per share in cash from its three largest institutional investors—SriSid, Arumilli, and Win-Light Global —to acquire all remaining outstanding shares of the company and transition ConnectM into a privately held entity.
  • The offer, submitted on March 31, 2025, implies a total equity valuation of approximately $46.5M for ConnectM.
  • SriSid, Arumilli, and Win-Light Global have been long-term investors in ConnectM, having initially invested in the company in 2020.
